# CAT-G-003 — Primary Sector Growth Fund (PSGF)

| Field | Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Funder** | Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI) |
| **Status** | **OPEN** for proposals (programme window listed to **2029-06-30** on GETS) |
| **URL** | https://www.mpi.govt.nz/funding-rural-support/primary-sector-growth-fund |
| **Replaces** | Sustainable Food & Fibre Futures (SFF Futures) — closed |
| **Model** | Co-investment removing barriers / capitalising on primary sector growth |
| **Fit score** | **88** — direct agritech / biosecurity / water / soil stack fit |

## Why it fits CAT

Domain portals are primary-sector problem solvers:

- **AquaGuard** — runoff, sediment, turbidity, consent support  
- **SoilGuard** — N-P-K, moisture, NES-F synthetic N context  
- **Sting-Operation-AI** — wasp/bee biosecurity vision  
- **Blue-Moon** — cultivation / kai production edge AI  
- **Sovereign-Edge-Firmware** — field sensor mesh  

Offline operation matches rural connectivity reality.

## Application shape (typical)

1. Early engagement with MPI / advisors  
2. Expression of Interest (problem, solution, economic benefit)  
3. Full proposal with co-investment plan  
4. Contracting + delivery reporting  

Expect multi-month lead time (often 6–12 months to approval).

## Suggested pilot EOI themes

1. **Catchment water intelligence** — AquaGuard mesh + local inference for regional council compliance support  
2. **Whenua nutrient stewardship** — SoilGuard actuator lockouts for regulatory safety  
3. **Apiary biosecurity sentinel** — Sting-Operation Hailo vision cluster  

## Co-funding

Plan cash + in-kind (farmer time, hardware, data labelling on-device). Confirm current PSGF ratio rules on MPI pages before locking budget.

## Next steps

1. Read PSGF about page + any current guidelines PDF  
2. Pick one portal + 2–3 pilot farms/orchards/hives  
3. Economic benefit model (yield, compliance cost avoided, labour)  
4. Draft EOI via grants-agent → HITL  
5. Email psgfund@mpi.govt.nz (confirm address on MPI site)

## Sources

- mpi.govt.nz PSGF pages
